raw_consumer_statement:
1. I had made a booking for XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX ( Booking # XXXX ) for XXXX XXXX ( Exhibit B1 ). 
1.1. I arrived at the Hotel location given on the XXXX booking ( Exhibit B2 ). The location is again very rural in a hilly area and has nothing resembling a hotel within miles of it. 
1.2. We called the Phone Number listed on the Listing, using my drivers phone ( since I didnt have an XXXX XXXX ). The gentleman who answered the phone said he used to be in the area as a worker, and someone has listed his phone number, and he regularly gets calls like this. If XXXX calls, he tells them to refund the money see attached call log screen capture ( Exhibit B3 ). 
1.3. I contacted XXXX customer service via their chat, but XXXX answered it and went unanswered for over 12 hours. Then the normal platitudes started off helping me ( like locking the stable after the horse had run away ). 
1.4. At a certain point, someone called XXXX jumped in ( I suspect a trained XXXX  robot ), who, under the guise of helping me, kept delaying the matter and then, after delaying the matter sufficiently, denied everything. XXXX XXXX, consumer XXXX. 
1.5. XXXX, according to their investigation, has verified that this hotel exists physically and legally. It has XXXX reviews, and XXXX hasnt answered how many total bookings they have received over the last 5 years. 
1.6. I have left a review for this hotel ( Exhibit B4 ). This review isnt published. 
1.7. In the meantime, XXXX has already charged me the money ( {$61.00} ) for a non-stay at a non-existent hotel ( Exhibit B5 ). 
1.8. I had to search for an alternate hotel about XXXX away in XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX # XXXX ) for which XXXX has also charged me. ( Exhibit B6 ). 
1.9. XXXX listing of this Fake hotel has interrupted my pilgrimage and caused me emotional, mental, and physical distress. 
1.10. I had to spend over 10 hours over next day going back to continue the partial pilgrimage , drive over extra XXXX km, and pay twice for the hotel.
